_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of data classification standards and methodologies based on sensitivity and other risk factors.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing data classification frameworks and implementing classification controls that enforce handling, access, and retention requirements based on data sensitivity and regulatory classification.
comments: DCWF ksa equal WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-005 (SP, cosine: 0.610), RM-K-001 (RM, cosine: 0.598).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.691). Shared vocabulary: [based, classification, data, sensitivity]. DCWF-only terms [factors, knowledge, methodologies, risk, standards] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, controls, designing, enforce, frameworks, handling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.896 (P=0.900, R=0.891). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of data classification standards and methodologies based on sensitivit" and "Skill in designing data classification frameworks and implementing classificatio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.667 ≈ STS 0.691 (gap 0.024). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [based, classification, data, sensitivity] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.1%. Shared tokens: [based, classification, data, sensitivity]. DCWF-only: [factors, knowledge, methodologies, risk, standards]. WIDAI-only: [access, controls, designing, enforce, frameworks, handling].
